title: Protocol: RNeasy Plant Mini Kit for RNA Extraction
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"7olmv","text":"This protocol describes the RNA extraction process from cannabis flowers using the Qiagen RNeasy Plant Mini Kit. Given the lack of a standardized RNA extraction method for cannabis, this protocol provides optimized steps to ensure high RNA yield and integrity by minimizing degradation. The workflow includes sample collection in the greenhouse (GH), flash-freezing in liquid nitrogen (LN2), mechanical disruption, and column-based RNA purification. Pre-warmed RNase-free water at 37°C is used to maximize RNA elution efficiency.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"4fvdf","text":"Work quickly to prevent RNA degradation.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"75btp","text":"Keep all tools and surfaces RNase-free by cleaning them with RNaseZap or 70% ethanol.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cnigh","text":"Use fresh gloves and sterile tools for each sample.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"k36b","text":"Flash-freeze all plant material immediately after liquid nitrogen or dry ice collection.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"auv9m","text":"Keep sample tubes and reagents cold during processing unless otherwise stated.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cnh9g","text":"Ensure ethanol (96-100%) is freshly prepared before use.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"4dmnj","text":"Do not allow tissue to thaw at any point before lysis.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8m0ah","text":"Buffer RLT and RW1 contain guanidine salts—avoid using with bleach or acid-based disinfectants.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"50p86","text":"Ethanol is flammable—keep away from flames and heat sources.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ddklm","text":"Wear protective equipment (gloves, lab coat, goggles) when handling β-mercaptoethanol.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9lcas","text":"Always use an RNA-dedicated pipette and work in an RNase-free area.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Grind the frozen material thoroughly with a mortar and pestle while keeping it in liquid nitrogen.
Transfer the finely powdered material to an RNase-free, pre-chilled 2 mL microcentrifuge tube (not supplied)
Set up a portable analytical balance in the GH
Label one tube per cultivar. Use 50 mL Falcon tubes
Bring RNase-free gloves and sterile tools (scissors, tweezers).
Cut fresh flowers using sterile scissors (one sample at a time)
Place the weighed ~100 mg sample into a labeled 50 mL Falcon tube. Poke some holes in lid of Falcon tube.
Flash-freeze the plant material (in 50 mL Falcon tube) immediately in liquid nitrogen
Add 450 µL Buffer RLT to the 2 mL with the plant material. Vortex (1 minute) vigorously for complete lysis. Make sure the sample looks homogenous (no visible chunks or fibrous material)
Transfer ALL the lysate ( ~ 450 µL) from the 2 mL tube to a QIAshredder spin column (lilac) placed in a 2 ml collection tube. Centrifuge for 2 min at full speed.
Add ~200 µL (0.5 volume of 100% ethanol ) to the cleared lysate and mix immediately by pipetting (DO NOT VORTEX) . Do not centrifuge. Proceed immediately to step 13.

Transfer the supernatant ( ~ 400 µL) of the flow-through to a new RNase-free 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube (not supplied) without disturbing the cell debris pellet in the collection tube. Use only this supernatant in subsequent steps. Discard only the QIAshredder spin column.
Transfer the sample (~600 µl) , including any precipitate that may have formed, to an RNeasy spin column (pink) placed in a 2 ml collection tube (supplied).
Close the lid and centrifuge for 15 seconds at ≥8000 x g (≥10,000 rpm). Discard the flow-through
Add 700 µl Buffer RW1 to the RNeasy spin column. Close the lid and centrifuge for 15 seconds at ≥8000 x g (≥10,000 rpm) to wash the spin column membrane. Discard the flow-through.
Add 500 µl Buffer RPE to the RNeasy spin column. Close the lid and centrifuge for 15 seconds at ≥8000 x g (≥10,000 rpm) to wash the spin column membrane. Discard the flow-through.
Add 500 µl Buffer RPE to the RNeasy spin column. Close the lid and centrifuge for 2 min at ≥8000 x g (≥10,000 rpm) to wash the spin column membrane.
Place the RNeasy spin column in a new 2 ml collection tube (supplied), and discard the old collection tube with the flow-through. Close the lid and centrifuge at full speed for 1 min.

Place the RNeasy spin column in a new 1.5 ml collection tube (supplied). Add 30 µl RNAse-free water directly to the spin column membrane. Let it sit for 1 minute at room temperature. Close the lid. Centrifuge for 1 min at ≥8000 x g (≥10,000 rpm) to elute the RNA.
Pre-warming the RNase-free water to 37°C in heat block
creator: Gonzalo Villarino
